Identities ❌ | Accounts ✔️ | Groups ❌ | App Roles ✔️ | Entitlements ❌

Application​

Steps

  1. Login to RSA G&L console as System Administrator.
  2. Navigate to Resources > Application.
  3. Click on Create Application and select Other Application.
  4. Enter the details as shown below.
  5. Click Finish.

Account Collector​

This section explains the process for configuring an account collector for the Oracle PeopleTools (PeopleSoft) application. We will use the SQL Server database collector for this purpose.

Steps

  1. Login to RSA G&L console as System Administrator.
  2. Navigate to Resources > Applications.
  3. Click on PeopleSoft.
  4. Navigate to Collectors tab and click on Create Account Collector. Enter the details as shown below.

  5. Click Next and enter the Oracle database details. Use the Test Connection to make sure the connection details are accurate.

  6. Click Next. Select the appropriate data that you intend to collect. For the purpose of illustration we will collect accounts, groups and user to account mapping from Oracle PeopleTools (PeopleSoft).

  7. Click Next. On the Account Data page, enter the following query to collect account information.
SELECT 
  DISTINCT REPLACE(
    upper(OPRID), 
    '''', 
    ''
  ) AS account, 
  EMPLID AS employeeID, 
  OPRDEFNDESC AS description, 
  LASTSIGNONDTTM AS lastLoginDate 
FROM 
  dbo.PSOPRDEFN‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍

The following are the important attributes

  1. Click Next. On the User Account Mappings Data configuration page enter the following query to map the PeopleSoft account to user identity.
SELECT 
  DISTINCT REPLACE(
    upper(OPRID), 
    '''', 
    ''
  ) AS account, 
  EMPLID AS employeeID 
FROM 
  dbo.PSOPRDEFN‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍

The following are the important attributes

  1. Click Next. In the User Resolution Rules, map the user to account

  2. Click Finish. Use the Test function to make sure the configurations are accurate.

Entitlement Collector​

This section explains the process for configuring an entitlement collector to collect admin roles from Oracle PeopleTools (PeopleSoft) application. We will use the SQL Server database collector for this purpose.

Steps

  1. Login to RSA G&L console as AveksaAdmin.
  2. Navigate to Resources > Applications.
  3. Click on PeopleSoft.
  4. Navigate to Collectors tab and click on Create Entitlement Collector. Enter the details as shown below.

  5. Click Next and enter the database connection details as shown in Step 5 of Account Collector.
  6. Click Next. Select the data types that the collector will be collecting from PeopleSoft.

  7. Click Next. On the General Column Names, map the attribute names.

  8. Click Next. Under Application Role Data section, use the following query to collect admin roles from PeopleSoft.
SELECT 
  DISTINCT ROLENAME AS role, 
  DESCR AS description 
FROM 
  dbo.PSROLEDEFN‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍

The following are the important attributes

  1. Click Next. On the Resource-Action Entitlements Data section, enter the query below to collect resource action mappings for roles.
SELECT 
  DISTINCT ROLENAME AS role, 
  CLASSID AS resource, 
  '' AS action 
FROM 
  dbo.PSROLECLASS 
union 
SELECT 
  DISTINCT '' AS role, 
  CLASSID AS resource, 
  '' AS action 
FROM 
  dbo.PSCLASSDEFN 
WHERE 
  CLASSID NOT IN(
    SELECT 
      DISTINCT CLASSID 
    FROM 
      dbo.PSROLECLASS
  )
  1. Click Next. On the Account Data section, enter the query below to collect mappings for account to roles.
SELECT 
  DISTINCT REPLACE(ROLEUSER, '''', '') AS account, 
  ROLENAME AS role 
FROM 
  dbo.PSROLEUSER‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍‍
  1. Click Next. In the User Evaluation, map the account to the ADC.
    Associated account collectorAccount value evaluates to
    Oracle PeopleTools (PeopleSoft) ADCAccount Name
  2. Click Next and then click Finish. Use the Test function to make sure the configurations are accurate.

item.title: Collections - MangoApps
item.description: MangoApps is a versatile collaboration platform that enables organizations to streamline communication and enhance productivity. It offers a comprehensive suite of features, including instant messaging, file sharing, task management, and team collaboration tools. With its intuitive interface and robust functionality, MangoApps empowers teams to connect, share ideas, and work together seamlessly across departments and geographies. Whether it's project coordination, knowledge sharing, or social engagement, MangoApps provides a unified platform that fosters efficient and effective collaboration within businesses of all sizes.MangoApps HomepageIdentities ❌ | Accounts ✔️ | Groups ✔️ | App Roles ❌ | Entitlements ❌
item.content:encoded:
MangoApps is a versatile collaboration platform that enables organizations to streamline communication and enhance productivity. It offers a comprehensive suite of features, including instant messaging, file sharing, task management, and team collaboration tools. With its intuitive interface and robust functionality, MangoApps empowers teams to connect, share ideas, and work together seamlessly across departments and geographies. Whether it's project coordination, knowledge sharing, or social engagement, MangoApps provides a unified platform that fosters efficient and effective collaboration within businesses of all sizes.

MangoApps Homepage

Identities ❌ | Accounts ✔️ | Groups ✔️ | App Roles ❌ | Entitlements ❌

Application​

Steps

  1. Login to RSA G&L console as System Administrator.
  2. Navigate to Resources > Application.
  3. Click on Create Application and select Other Application.
  4. Enter the details as shown below.
  5. Click Finish.

Account Collector​

This section explains the process for configuring an account collector for the MangoApps application. We will use the RSA G&L Generic REST collector for this purpose.

Steps

  1. Login to RSA G&L console as System Administrator.
  2. Navigate to Resources > Applications.
  3. Click on MangoApps.
  4. Navigate to Collectors tab and click on Create Account Collector. Enter the details as shown below.

  5. Click Next and enter the connection details. Refer to Token Authentication for configuring the authentication for this collector.

    .
  6. Click Next. Select the data types that the collector will be collecting from MangoApps

  7. Click Next. On the Account Data page, enter the following configurations.




    Request
    MethodGET
    URL/api/users.json
    Request Headers
    Acceptapplication/json
    AuthorizationBearer ${AuthenticationToken}
    Response Mappings
    AccountId$.ms_response.users.*.email
    Id$.ms_response.users.*.id
    EmployeeId$.ms_response.users.*.employee_id
    UserType$.ms_response.users.*.user_type
  8. Click Next. On the Group Data page, enter the following configurations.




    Request
    MethodGET
    URL/api/groups.json
    Request Headers
    Acceptapplication/json
    AuthorizationBearer ${AuthenticationToken}
    Response Mappings
    GroupId$.ms_response.groups.*.name
    Id$.ms_response.groups.*.id
  9. Click Next. On the Account Group Membership page, enter the following configurations.




    Request
    MethodGET
    URL/api/conversations/${Id}/members.json
    Request Headers
    Acceptapplication/json
    AuthorizationBearer ${AuthenticationToken}
    Response Mappings
    AccountId$.ms_response.members.*.email
  10. Click Next. In the Map Collector Attributes to Account Attributes, map the collected account attributes.

  11. Click Next. In the Map Collector Attributes to Account Mapping Attributes, map the collected group attributes.

  12. Click Next. In the Map Collector Attributes to Group Attributes, map the collected group attributes.

  13. Click Next. In the User Resolution Rules, map the user to account.

  14. Click Next. In the Member Account Resolution Rules, map the account attribute.

  15. Click Finish. Use the Test function to make sure the configurations are accurate.

Application Setup​

This section explains the process of acquiring the authentication data required for collectors and connectors from MangoApps

Steps

  1. Login to MangoApps Admin Portal.
  2. Navigate to Integration > Webhooks
  3. Note down the Client ID and Client Secret

  4. Navigate to Users > Add Users to add a new service account user. If you already have a user account, proceed.
  5. For collections, a normal user account with no roles can be used. However, for provisioning (e.g. enable / disable accounts) the service accounts needs to have Network Admin role. Navigate to Users > Admin Roles.
  6. Under Roles, select Network Admin.
  7. Click Add Admin and assign the user by clicking Make Network Admin.

Token Authentication​

This section explains the process of setting up RSA G&L ADC with token authentication.

Steps

  1. Login to RSA G&L console as System Administrator.
  2. Create or Edit a generic REST ADC that will be using Token authentication.
  3. On the Connection Details page, configure the following.



    Request
    MethodPOST
    Token URLhttps://instance.mangoapps.com/oauth2/token.json
    Usernameuser@company.com
    PasswordUser Account Password
    Request Headers
    Acceptapplication/json
    Content-Typeapplication/x-www-form-urlencoded
    Request Body
    client_id=CLIENT_ID&client_secret=CLIENT_SECRET&grant_type=password&username=${TokenUsername}&password=${TokenPassword}
    Response Mappings
    Response Path$.access_token
  4. Save the collector configuration & test.

item.title: Collections - DocuSign
item.description: DocuSign is a leading electronic signature platform that streamlines document signing processes. Trusted by businesses globally, it enables secure, efficient, and legally binding digital transactions. Users can electronically sign, send, and manage documents from any device, reducing paperwork and enhancing productivity. DocuSign's robust features ensure compliance and accelerate workflows, making it an essential tool for modern business transactions.Identities ❌ | Accounts ✔️ | Groups ✔️ | App Roles ✔️ | Entitlements ❌
item.content:encoded:
DocuSign is a leading electronic signature platform that streamlines document signing processes. Trusted by businesses globally, it enables secure, efficient, and legally binding digital transactions. Users can electronically sign, send, and manage documents from any device, reducing paperwork and enhancing productivity. DocuSign's robust features ensure compliance and accelerate workflows, making it an essential tool for modern business transactions.

Identities ❌ | Accounts ✔️ | Groups ✔️ | App Roles ✔️ | Entitlements ❌

Users are individuals who have accounts on the DocuSign platform. Each user has a unique login and profile within the system. Users can send, sign, and manage documents based on the permissions and access levels granted to them. Users can be assigned to specific groups and assigned permission profiles to define their capabilities within the platform.

Groups are collections of users that allow you to manage permissions and access for multiple users simultaneously. Instead of setting permissions for each individual user, you can assign permissions to a group, and all users within that group inherit those permissions. This makes it easier to manage access rights for teams or departments. For example, you might have a "Sales Team" group with permissions tailored to sales-related tasks.

Permission profiles, also known as permission sets, are sets of rules that define what actions users and groups are allowed to perform within the DocuSign platform. These profiles control the level of access to various features and functionalities. Permission profiles can include actions like sending envelopes, managing templates, accessing specific folders, or even administrative tasks. By assigning different permission profiles to users or groups, you can ensure that each user has the appropriate level of access based on their role and responsibilities.

Application​

Steps

  1. Login to RSA G&L console as System Administrator.
  2. Navigate to Resources > Application.
  3. Click on Create Application and select Other Application.
  4. Enter the details as shown below.
  5. Click Finish.

Account Collector​

This section explains the process for configuring an account collector for the DocuSign application. We will use the RSA G&L Generic REST collector for this purpose.

Steps

  1. Login to RSA G&L console as System Administrator.
  2. Navigate to Resources > Applications.
  3. Click on DocuSign.
  4. Navigate to Collectors tab and click on Create Account Collector. Enter the details as shown below.

  5. Click Next and enter the connection details.


    info

    Here since I am using demo instance the Base URI is https://demo.docusign.net. Verify the Base URI instance for your environment by calling the User Info API.

    For more information, refer to https://developers.docusign.com/platform/auth/reference/user-info/

  6. If authentication type is OAuth, refer to OAuth2 Configuration with RSA G&L section.
  7. Click Next. Select the data types that the collector will be collecting from DocuSign

  8. Click Next. On the Account Data page, enter the following configurations.




    Request
    MethodGET
    URL/Users
    Request Headers
    Acceptapplication/json
    AuthorizationBearer ${AuthenticationToken}
    Response Mappings
    AccountId$.users.*.email
    Id$.users.*.userId
    UserType$.users.*.userType
    UserStatus$.users.*.userStatus

  9. Click Next. On the Group Data page, enter the following configurations.




    Request
    MethodGET
    URL/groups
    Request Headers
    Acceptapplication/json
    AuthorizationBearer ${AuthenticationToken}
    Response Mappings
    GroupId$.groups.*.groupName
    Id$.groups.*.groupId
    Type$.groups.*.groupType
  10. Click Next. On the Account Group Membership page, enter the following configurations.




    Request
    MethodGET
    URL/groups/${Id}/users
    Request Headers
    Acceptapplication/json
    AuthorizationBearer ${AuthenticationToken}
    Response Mappings
    Account Identifier$.users.*.userId
  11. Click Next. In the Map Collector Attributes to Account Attributes, map the collected account attributes.

  12. Click Next. In the Map Collector Attributes to Account Mapping Attributes, map the user reference attribute.

  13. Click Next. In the Map Collector Attributes to Group Attributes, map the collected group attributes.

  14. Click Next. In the User Resolution Rules, map the user to account.

  15. Click Next. In the Member Account Resolution Rules, map the account.

💡 Info: This value MUST always be mapped to the Account Name for any generic REST collector.

  1. Click Finish. Use the Test function to make sure the configurations are accurate.

Entitlement Collector​

This section explains the process for configuring an entitlement collector to collect permission profiles from DocuSign application. We will use the RSA G&L Generic REST collector for this purpose.

Steps

  1. Login to RSA G&L console as AveksaAdmin.
  2. Navigate to Resources > Applications.
  3. Click on DocuSign.
  4. Navigate to Collectors tab and click on Create Entitlement Collector. Enter the details as shown below.

  5. Click Next and enter the connection details as shown in Step 5 of Account Collector.
  6. Click Next. Select the data types that the collector will be collecting from DocuSign.

  7. Click Next. On the App Role Data page, enter the following configurations.




    Request
    MethodGET
    URL/permission_profiles
    Request Headers
    Acceptapplication/json
    AuthorizationBearer ${AuthenticationToken}
    Response Mappings
    ApplicationRoleId$.permissionProfiles.*.permissionProfileName
    Id$.permissionProfiles.*.permissionProfileId
  8. Click Next. On the App-Role Account Membership collection page, enter the following configurations.




    Request
    MethodGET
    URL/Users
    Request Headers
    Acceptapplication/json
    AuthorizationBearer ${AuthenticationToken}
    Response Mappings
    Application Role Id$.users.*.permissionProfileId
    Account Identifier$.users.*.userId
  9. Click Next. In the Map Collector Attributes to App Role Attributes, map the collected account attributes.
  10. Click Next. In the Account Evaluation, map the account collector to account value.

  11. Click Next and then click Finish. Use the Test function to make sure the configurations are accurate.

Application Setup​

This section explains the process of setting up a service account in DocuSign for use within the collections process.

Steps

  1. Login to DocuSign eSignature portal.
  2. From the main menu, click on Integrations > Apps and Keys. 
  3. Click on ADD APP AND INTEGRATION KEY button.
  4. Provide a suitable App Name and click Create App
  5. Copy the Integration Key, which will be used as the Client ID in OAuth authentication.
  6. Under Authentication, click on Add Secret Key. Copy the generated secret key and retain it for use as Client Secret in OAuth authentication.
  7. Specify the OAuth callback URL (https://G&L_HOST_NAME:8443/aveksa/oauth/callback) under Redirect URIs.
  8. Under Allowed HTTP Methods, check GET, since we only need permissions for GET API calls.

  9. Click Save.

OAuth2 Configuration​

This section explains the process of setting up RSA G&L ADC with OAuth2 authentication.

Steps

  1. Login to RSA G&L console as System Administrator.
  2. Create or Edit a generic REST ADC that will be using OAuth2 authentication.
  3. On the Connection Details page, configure the following.

    Request
    Client ID-
    Client Secret-
    Authentication URLhttps://account-d.docusign.com/oauth/auth (Development)
    https://account.docusign.com/oauth/auth (Production)
    Access Token URLhttps://account-d.docusign.com/oauth/token (Development)
    https://account.docusign.com/oauth/token (Production)
    Scopesignature extended
  4. Click on Get OAuth 2.0 Access Token.
  5. Click on Proceed For Authorization.

  6. Login to DocuSign account.

  7. The below message indicates that RSA G&L has successfully acquired a token.

  8. Click Close.
  9. Save the collector configuration. Use the Test function to make sure the configurations are accurate

item.title: Collections - Zendesk
item.description: Zendesk is a customer support platform that streamlines interactions between businesses and their customers. It offers ticket management, knowledge base, live chat, and more, empowering organizations to deliver exceptional support experiences. With user-friendly features, Zendesk enhances customer communication and issue resolution, ultimately fostering strong relationships and improving customer satisfaction.Identities ❌ | Accounts ✔️ | Groups ✔️ | App Roles ❌ | Entitlements ❌
item.content:encoded:
Zendesk is a customer support platform that streamlines interactions between businesses and their customers. It offers ticket management, knowledge base, live chat, and more, empowering organizations to deliver exceptional support experiences. With user-friendly features, Zendesk enhances customer communication and issue resolution, ultimately fostering strong relationships and improving customer satisfaction.

Identities ❌ | Accounts ✔️ | Groups ✔️ | App Roles ❌ | Entitlements ❌

Application​

Steps

  1. Login to RSA G&L console as System Administrator.
  2. Navigate to Resources > Application.
  3. Click on Create Application and select Other Application.
  4. Enter the details as shown below.
  5. Click Finish.

Account Collector​

This section explains the process for configuring an account collector for the Zendesk application. We will use the RSA G&L Generic REST collector for this purpose.

Steps

  1. Login to RSA G&L console as System Administrator.
  2. Navigate to Resources > Applications.
  3. Click on Zendesk.
  4. Navigate to Collectors tab and click on Create Account Collector. Enter the details as shown below.

  5. Click Next and enter the connection details.Since we are using Token Access method for authentication, the collector will use Basic authentication type.

    info

    The service account email address needs to be prefixed with /token and password will be the API token.

    Refer to [Security and authentication : API Token](https://developer.zendesk.com/api-reference/introduction/security-and-auth/#api-token) for more information

  6. Click Next. Select the data types that the collector will be collecting from Zendesk

  7. Click Next. On the Account Data page, enter the following configurations.



    Pagination - Offset Method



    Pagination - Cursor Method



    Request
    MethodGET
    URL/api/v2/users
    Query Parameters
    role[]admin
    role[]agent
    Request Headers
    Acceptapplication/json
    Response Mappings
    AccountId$.users.*.email
    Id$.users.*.id
    LastLogin$.users.*.last_login_at
    Verified$.users.*.verified
    IsActive$.users.*.active

  8. Click Next. On the Group Data page, enter the following configurations.



    Pagination - Offset Method



    Pagination - Cursor Method



    Request
    MethodGET
    URL/api/v2/groups
    Request Headers
    Acceptapplication/json
    Response Mappings
    GroupId$.groups.*.name
    Id$.groups.*.id
    Description$.groups.*.description
  9. Click Next. On the Account Group Membership page, enter the following configurations.



    Request
    MethodGET
    URL/api/v2/group_memberships
    Request Headers
    Acceptapplication/json
    Response Mappings
    AccountId$.*.profile.login
  10. Click Next. In the Map Collector Attributes to Account Attributes, map the collected account attributes.

  11. Click Next. In the Map Collector Attributes to Group Attributes, map the collected group attributes.

  12. Click Next. In the Map Collector Attributes to Group Attributes, map the collected group attributes.

  13. Click Next. In the User Resolution Rules, map the user to account.

  14. Click Next. In the Member Account Resolution Rules, map the account attribute.

  15. Click Finish. Use the Test function to make sure the configurations are accurate.

Application Setup​

This section explains the process of setting up a service account in Zendesk for use within the collections process.

Steps

  1. Login to Zendesk admin dashboard Apps and integrations > APIs > Zendesk API.
  2. If you are accessing for the first time, agree to terms and conditions.
  3. Enable the Token Access.

  4. Click on Add API token.

  5. Provide a suitable name and copy the token for use with G&L.
  6. Click Save.

item.title: Collections - Okta
item.description: Okta is a cloud-based IAM platform that offers secure user authentication, SSO, and authorization. It simplifies identity management, access control, and provisioning for organizations. With Okta, businesses can enhance security, streamline user experiences, and enable seamless access to applications and data. It supports multifactor authentication and integrations with various systems, enabling secure digital transformation and centralized identity management.Identities ❌ | Accounts ✔️ | Groups ✔️ | App Roles ✔️ | Entitlements ❌
item.content:encoded:
Okta is a cloud-based IAM platform that offers secure user authentication, SSO, and authorization. It simplifies identity management, access control, and provisioning for organizations. With Okta, businesses can enhance security, streamline user experiences, and enable seamless access to applications and data. It supports multifactor authentication and integrations with various systems, enabling secure digital transformation and centralized identity management.

Identities ❌ | Accounts ✔️ | Groups ✔️ | App Roles ✔️ | Entitlements ❌

Users - Okta allows you to create and manage user accounts, including attributes like name, email, and profile information. Users can be assigned to various groups and roles.

Groups - Okta enables the creation of groups to organize users based on common attributes or roles. Groups help streamline user management and permissions assignment.

Group Members - Okta allows you to add users as members of specific groups. This helps control access to resources and streamline user provisioning and management.

Roles - Okta supports the definition and assignment of roles. Roles represent a collection of permissions or access privileges that can be granted to users or groups.

Role Members - Okta enables the assignment of users or groups as members of specific roles. This allows for fine-grained access control and authorization based on role-based permissions.

Application​

Steps

  1. Login to RSA G&L console as System Administrator.
  2. Navigate to Resources > Application.
  3. Click on Create Application and select Other Application.
  4. Enter the details as shown below.
  5. Click Finish.

Account Collector​

This section explains the process for configuring an account collector for the Okta application. We will use the RSA G&L Generic REST collector for this purpose.

Steps

  1. Login to RSA G&L console as System Administrator.
  2. Navigate to Resources > Applications.
  3. Click on Okta.
  4. Navigate to Collectors tab and click on Create Account Collector. Enter the details as shown below.

  5. Click Next and enter the connection details.

  6. Click Next. Select the data types that the collector will be collecting from Okta

  7. Click Next. On the Account Data page, enter the following configurations.



    info

    The pagination model of Okta is currently not supported by REST collectors. The default page size for Okta REST APIs is 200 user records.

    Request
    MethodGET
    URL/api/v1/users
    Request Headers
    Acceptapplication/json
    AuthorizationSSWS ${AuthenticationToken}
    Response Mappings
    AccountId$.*.profile.login
    Id$.*.id
    Status$.*.status
    LastLogin$.*.lastLogin
    EmployeeNumber$.*.profile.employeeNumber

  8. Click Next. On the Group Data page, enter the following configurations.




    info

    The pagination model of Okta is currently not supported by REST collectors. The default page size for Okta REST APIs is 10000 group records.

    In our example, the Okta groups are AD based and hence we will be collecting only Okta Groups (local) using the filter. We will collect AD groups to entitlements relationship directly in EDC

    Request
    MethodGET
    URL/api/v1/groups
    Query Parameters
    filtertype eq "OKTA_GROUP"
    Request Headers
    Acceptapplication/json
    AuthorizationSSWS ${AuthenticationToken}
    Response Mappings
    GroupId$.*.profile.name
    Id$.*.id
    Description$.*.profile.description
    Type$.*.type
  9. Click Next. On the Account Group Membership page, enter the following configurations.



    info

    The pagination model of Okta is currently not supported by REST collectors. The default page size for Okta REST APIs is 1000 group member records.

    Request
    MethodGET
    URL/api/v1/groups/${Id}/users
    Request Headers
    Acceptapplication/json
    AuthorizationSSWS ${AuthenticationToken}
    Response Mappings
    AccountId$.*.profile.login
  10. Click Next. In the Map Collector Attributes to Account Attributes, map the collected account attributes.

  11. Click Next. In the Map Collector Attributes to Group Attributes, map the collected group attributes.

  12. Click Next. In the Map Collector Attributes to Group Attributes, map the collected group attributes.

  13. Click Next. In the User Resolution Rules, map the user to account.

  14. Click Next. In the Member Account Resolution Rules, map the account attribute.

  15. Click Finish. Use the Test function to make sure the configurations are accurate.

Entitlement Collector​

This section explains the process for configuring an entitlement collector to collect applications from Okta. We will use the RSA G&L Generic REST collector for this purpose.

Steps

  1. Login to RSA G&L console as AveksaAdmin.
  2. Navigate to Resources > Applications.
  3. Click on Okta.
  4. Navigate to Collectors tab and click on Create Entitlement Collector. Enter the details as shown below.

  5. Click Next and enter the connection details as shown in Step 5 of Account Collector.
  6. Click Next. Select the data types that the collector will be collecting from Okta.

  7. Click Next. On the App Role Data page, enter the following configurations.



    Request
    MethodGET
    URL/api/v1/apps
    Request Headers
    Acceptapplication/json
    AuthorizationSSWS ${AuthenticationToken}
    Response Mappings
    ApplicationRoleId$.*.label
    Id$.*.id
    Name$.*.name
    Status$.*.status
  8. Click Next. On the App-Role Account Membership collection page, enter the following configurations.



    Request
    MethodGET
    URL/api/v1/apps/${Id}/users
    Request Headers
    Acceptapplication/json
    AuthorizationSSWS ${AuthenticationToken}
    Response Mappings
    Account Identifier$.*.id
  9. Click Next. On the App-Role Group Membership collection page, enter the following configurations.



    Request
    MethodGET
    URL/api/v1/apps/${Id}/groups
    Request Headers
    Acceptapplication/json
    AuthorizationSSWS ${AuthenticationToken}
    Response Mappings
    Group Identifier$.value.*.memberName
  10. Click Next. In the Map Collector Attributes to App Role Attributes, map the collected account attributes.
  11. Click Next. In the Account Evaluation, map the account collector to account value.

  12. Click Next. In the Group Evaluation, map the account collector to group value.

  13. Click Next and then click Finish. Use the Test function to make sure the configurations are accurate

Application Setup​

This section explains the process of setting up a service account in Okta for use within the collections process.

Steps

  1. Login to Okta dashboard, https://instance.okta.com/admin/dashboard.
  2. From the main menu, navigate to Security > API. 
  3. Under Tokens tab, click on Create token button.
  4. Provide a suitable name and click Create token.
  5. Copy the token and retain it for use with G&L.
  6. Click OK.

item.title: Collections - Lucid
item.description: Lucidchart is a web-based diagramming and visualization tool that enables users to create flowcharts, diagrams, mind maps, and more. It offers collaborative features for teams to work together in real-time, enhancing communication and productivity. With an intuitive interface and various templates, Lucidchart simplifies complex concepts into clear visual representations.Identities ❌ | Accounts ✔️ | Groups ❌ | App Roles ✔️ | Entitlements ❌
item.content:encoded:
Lucidchart is a web-based diagramming and visualization tool that enables users to create flowcharts, diagrams, mind maps, and more. It offers collaborative features for teams to work together in real-time, enhancing communication and productivity. With an intuitive interface and various templates, Lucidchart simplifies complex concepts into clear visual representations.

Identities ❌ | Accounts ✔️ | Groups ❌ | App Roles ✔️ | Entitlements ❌

Application​

Steps

  1. Login to RSA G&L console as System Administrator.
  2. Navigate to Resources > Application.
  3. Click on Create Application and select Other Application.
  4. Enter the details as shown below.
  5. Click Finish.

Account Collector​

This section explains the process for configuring an account collector for the Lucid application. We will use the RSA G&L Generic REST collector for this purpose.

Steps

  1. Login to RSA G&L console as System Administrator.
  2. Navigate to Resources > Applications.
  3. Click on Lucid.
  4. Navigate to Collectors tab and click on Create Account Collector. Enter the details as shown below.

  5. Click Next and enter the connection details.

    .
    💡 Info: Lucid only supports OAuth authentication type. Refer to Click OAuth2 Configuration with RSA G&L for more information.
  6. Click Next. Select the data types that the collector will be collecting from Lucid

  7. Click Next. On the Account Data page, enter the following configurations.


    Request
    MethodGET
    URL/users
    Request Headers
    Acceptapplication/json
    AuthorizationBearer ${AuthenticationToken}
    Lucid-Api-Version1
    Response Mappings
    AccountId$.*.email
    Id$.*.userId
    UserName$.*.username

  8. Click Next. In the Map Collector Attributes to Account Attributes, map the collected account attributes.

  9. Click Next. In the Map Collector Attributes to Account Mapping Attributes, map the user reference attribute.

  10. Click Next. In the User Resolution Rules, map the user to account.

  11. Click Finish. Use the Test function to make sure the configurations are accurate.

Entitlement Collector​

This section explains the process for configuring an entitlement collector to collect site roles and assignments from the Lucidchart application. We will use the RSA G&L Generic REST collector for this purpose.

info

Since Lucid does not provide an API for listing admin roles, we will use a custom web service for the EDC. Refer to Custom Web Service : Site Roles for more information.

Steps

  1. Login to RSA G&L console as AveksaAdmin.
  2. Navigate to Resources > Applications.
  3. Click on Lucid.
  4. Navigate to Collectors tab and click on Create Entitlement Collector. Enter the details as shown below.

  5. Click Next and enter the connection details as shown in Step 5 of Account Collector. 💡 Note that the Use Base URL for all API(s) will be unchecked.
  6. Click Next. Select the data types that the collector will be collecting from Lucid.

  7. Click Next. On the App Role Data page, enter the following configurations.



    Request
    MethodGET
    URLhttps://instance.securid.com/aveksa/custom/external_jsp/LucidAdminRolesv1.jsp
    Request Headers
    Acceptapplication/json
    Response Mappings
    ApplicationRoleId$.roles.*.role
    Description$.roles.*.description
  8. Click Next. On the App-Role Account Membership collection page, enter the following configurations.



    Request
    MethodGET
    URLhttps://api.lucid.co/users
    Request Headers
    Acceptapplication/json
    AuthorizationBearer ${AuthenticationToken}
    Lucid-Api-Version1
    Response Mappings
    Application Role Id$.*.roles
    Account Identifier$.*.userId
  9. Click Next. In the Map Collector Attributes to App Role Attributes, map the collected account attributes.

  10. Click Next. In the Account Evaluation, map the account collector to account value.

  11. Click Next and then click Finish. Use the Test function to make sure the configurations are accurate

Application Setup​

This section explains the process of setting up Lucid for integration with G&L.

Steps

  1. Login to Lucid Developer Console, https://admin.lucid.app/developer. You need to have Developer Tools enabled by the Lucid admin for your organization.
  2. Click on New Application.

  3. Provide a suitable name and click Create.

  4. Click on OAuth 2.0 tab.

  5. Provide a client name and click Create OAuth 2.0 client.

  6. Copy the client id and client secret.

  7. Click on Add Redirect URI and add the G&L OAuth call back URL https://instance.securid.com/aveksa/oauth/callback.

OAuth2 Configuration​

This section explains the process of setting up RSA G&L ADC with token authentication.

Steps

  1. Login to RSA G&L console as System Administrator.
  2. Create or Edit a generic REST ADC that will be using OAuth2 authentication.
  3. On the Connection Details page, configure the following.


    Request
    Client ID-
    Client Secret-
    Authentication URLhttps://lucid.app/oauth2/authorizeAccount
    Access Token URLhttps://api.lucid.co/oauth2/token
    Scopeoffline_access account.user:readonly
  4. Click on Get OAuth 2.0 Access Token.
  5. Click on Proceed For Authorization.

  6. Login to Lucid account.

  7. The below message indicates that RSA G&L has successfully acquired a token.

  8. Click Close.
  9. Save the collector configuration. Use the Test function to make sure the configurations are accurate

Custom Web Service : Admin Roles​

Since Lucid does not provide an API for listing admin roles, we will use a custom JSP that will publish the site roles.

Steps

  1. Open a text editor and save the contents below as LucidAdminRolesv1.jsp.
<%@ page language="java" contentType="application/json" %>
<%
    String jsonData = "{"
        + "\"roles\": ["
        + "{\"role\":\"billing-admin\",\"description\":\"Billing admins can change payment options for the account.\"},"
        + "{\"role\":\"document-approver\",\"description\":\"Document approvers can approve documents.\"},"
        + "{\"role\":\"team-admin\",\"description\":\"Team admins can access and edit team settings, including user roles and groups.\"}"
        + "]"
        + "}";
%>

<%
    response.setContentType("application/json");
    out.print(jsonData);
%>
  1. Login to RSA G&L console as AveksaAdmin.
  2. Navigate to Admin > User Interface.
  3. Click on Files.
  4. Select External JSP Pages and click on Upload.
  5. Select the LucidAdminRolesv1.jsp and click OK.
  6. Verify by accessing this URL in the browser https://instance.securid.com/aveksa/custom/external_jsp/LucidAdminRolesv1.jsp

item.title: Collections - Datadog
item.description: Datadog is a monitoring and analytics platform that helps organizations gain insights into their applications and infrastructure. It offers real-time visibility, alerting, and visualization tools to monitor performance, troubleshoot issues, and optimize resources across cloud, on-premises, and hybrid environments. With its user-friendly interface, Datadog empowers teams to ensure system reliability and improve overall operational efficiency.Identities ❌ | Accounts ✔️ | Groups ✔️ | App Roles ✔️ | Entitlements ❌
item.content:encoded:
Datadog is a monitoring and analytics platform that helps organizations gain insights into their applications and infrastructure. It offers real-time visibility, alerting, and visualization tools to monitor performance, troubleshoot issues, and optimize resources across cloud, on-premises, and hybrid environments. With its user-friendly interface, Datadog empowers teams to ensure system reliability and improve overall operational efficiency.

Identities ❌ | Accounts ✔️ | Groups ✔️ | App Roles ✔️ | Entitlements ❌

Users are individuals who interact with Datadog to monitor and manage applications and infrastructure. Each user has a unique login and can access various features based on their assigned roles and permissions. Users can create and customize dashboards, set up alerts, analyze data, and contribute to overall monitoring efforts.

Teams are groups of users that work together on specific projects, departments, or functions. Teams provide a way to organize and streamline access to resources within Datadog. By grouping users into teams, administrators can efficiently manage permissions, access control, and data visibility. Teams enhance collaboration by allowing members to collectively contribute to monitoring tasks and share insights.

Roles define the level of access and permissions that users and teams have within Datadog. Different roles grant varying degrees of control over the platform's features and data. For example, roles can include "Admin" with full access and management privileges, "Read-Only" with view-only capabilities, or custom roles tailored to specific needs. Roles ensure that users and teams have appropriate levels of control and responsibility while maintaining security and data integrity.

Application​

Steps

  1. Login to RSA G&L console as System Administrator.
  2. Navigate to Resources > Application.
  3. Click on Create Application and select Other Application.
  4. Enter the details as shown below.
  5. Click Finish.

Account Collector​

This section explains the process for configuring an account collector for the Datadog application. We will use the RSA G&L Generic REST collector API for this purpose.

Steps

  1. Login to RSA G&L console as System Administrator.
  2. Navigate to Resources > Applications.
  3. Click on Datadog.
  4. Navigate to Collectors tab and click on Create Account Collector. Enter the details as shown below.

  5. Click Next and enter the connection details.

  6. Click Next. Select the data types that the collector will be collecting from Datadog

  7. Click Next. On the Account Data page, enter the following configurations.




    Request
    MethodGET
    URL/api/v2/users
    Request Headers
    Acceptapplication/json
    DD-API-KEY${AuthenticationToken}
    DD-APPLICATION-KEYAPPLICATION_KEY
    Response Mappings
    AccountId$.data.*.attributes.email
    Id$.data.*.id
    Status$.data.*.attributes.status

  8. Click Next. On the Group Data page, enter the following configurations.




    Request
    MethodGET
    URL/api/v2/team
    Request Headers
    Acceptapplication/json
    DD-API-KEY${AuthenticationToken}
    DD-APPLICATION-KEYAPPLICATION_KEY
    Response Mappings
    GroupId$.data.*.attributes.name
    Id$.data.*.id
    Description$.data.*.attributes.description
  9. Click Next. On the Account Group Membership page, enter the following configurations.




    Request
    MethodGET
    URL/api/v2/team/${Id}/memberships
    Request Headers
    Acceptapplication/json
    DD-API-KEY${AuthenticationToken}
    DD-APPLICATION-KEYAPPLICATION_KEY
    Response Mappings
    Account Identifier$.data.*.relationships.user.data.id
  10. Click Next. In the Map Collector Attributes to Account Attributes, map the collected account attributes.

  11. Click Next. In the Map Collector Attributes to Account Mapping Attributes, map the user reference attribute.

  12. Click Next. In the Map Collector Attributes to Group Attributes, map the collected group attributes.

  13. Click Next. In the User Resolution Rules, map the user to account.

  14. Click Next. In the Member Account Resolution Rules, map the account.

💡 Info: This value MUST always be mapped to the Account Name for any generic REST collector.

  1. Click Finish. Use the Test function to make sure the configurations are accurate.

Entitlement Collector​

This section explains the process for configuring an entitlement collector to collect user roles and assignments from the Datadog application. We will use the RSA G&L Generic REST collector for this purpose.

Steps

  1. Login to RSA G&L console as AveksaAdmin.
  2. Navigate to Resources > Applications.
  3. Click on Datadog.
  4. Navigate to Collectors tab and click on Create Entitlement Collector. Enter the details as shown below.

  5. Click Next and enter the connection details as shown in Step 5 of Account Collector.
  6. Click Next. Select the data types that the collector will be collecting from Datadog.

  7. Click Next. On the App Role Data page, enter the following configurations.




    Request
    MethodGET
    URL/api/v2/roles
    Request Headers
    Acceptapplication/json
    DD-API-KEY${AuthenticationToken}
    DD-APPLICATION-KEYAPPLICATION_KEY
    Response Mappings
    ApplicationRoleId$.data.*.attributes.name
    Id$.data.*.id
  8. Click Next. On the App-Role Account Membership collection page, enter the following configurations.




    Request
    MethodGET
    URL/api/v2/roles/${Id}/users
    Request Headers
    Acceptapplication/json
    DD-API-KEY${AuthenticationToken}
    DD-APPLICATION-KEYAPPLICATION_KEY
    Response Mappings
    Account Identifier$.data.*.id
  9. Click Next. In the Map Collector Attributes to App Role Attributes, map the collected account attributes.

  10. Click Next. In the Account Evaluation, map the account collector to account value.

  11. Click Next and then click Finish. Use the Test function to make sure the configurations are accurate

Application Setup​

This section explains the process of setting up Datadog for integration with G&L. Datadog API calls require application key and API key for most invocations.

Steps

  1. Login to Datadog console and navigate to organization settings, https://us5.datadoghq.com/organization-settings.
  2. Under Access, click on Application Keys.
  3. Click on New Key.
  4. Provide a suitable name and click Create Key.
  5. Copy the key value. We will refer to this as APPLICATION_KEY
  6. Click on Edit next to Scope. Select the scopes as shown below.

  7. Click Save and then click Finish.
  8. Under Access, click on API Keys.
  9. Click on New Key.
  10. Copy the key value and click Finish.
  1. API Reference Docs

    ↩

item.title: Collections - PagerDuty
item.description: PagerDuty is a leading digital operations management platform that empowers teams to proactively manage and respond to incidents in real time. By providing actionable insights, automation, and collaboration tools, PagerDuty ensures business continuity and exceptional customer experiences. It centralizes alerts, streamlines incident resolution, and enables teams to work seamlessly across the entire organization, minimizing downtime and optimizing operational efficiency.Identities ❌ | Accounts ✔️ | Groups ✔️ | App Roles ✔️ | Entitlements ❌
item.content:encoded:
PagerDuty is a leading digital operations management platform that empowers teams to proactively manage and respond to incidents in real time. By providing actionable insights, automation, and collaboration tools, PagerDuty ensures business continuity and exceptional customer experiences. It centralizes alerts, streamlines incident resolution, and enables teams to work seamlessly across the entire organization, minimizing downtime and optimizing operational efficiency.

Identities ❌ | Accounts ✔️ | Groups ✔️ | App Roles ✔️ | Entitlements ❌

Teams are a way to organize users and objects in PagerDuty. They can be used to control who has access to different objects, such as escalation policies, users, schedules, and services. Teams can also be used to customize the PagerDuty web app by filtering for information relevant to the team's associated objects.

User roles define the level of access that a user has to PagerDuty objects.

RoleDescription
adminHas full access to all objects and configurations in the account. Can create, update, and delete users, teams, schedules, escalation policies, services, and integrations. Can also manage user permissions.
limited_userCan view objects and configurations, but cannot make any modifications.
observerCan view objects, but cannot make any modifications.
ownerHas full access to objects and configurations within their team. Can create, update, and delete users, schedules, escalation policies, and services. Can also manage user permissions within their team.
read_only_userCan view objects, but cannot make any modifications.
restricted_accessCan only view incidents and alerts that they are directly involved in.
read_only_limited_userCan only view objects that they have been granted access to.
userCan view objects and configurations, but cannot make any modifications.

Application​

Steps

  1. Login to RSA G&L console as System Administrator.
  2. Navigate to Resources > Application.
  3. Click on Create Application and select Other Application.
  4. Enter the details as shown below.
  5. Click Finish.

Account Collector​

This section explains the process for configuring an account collector for the PagerDuty application. We will use the RSA G&L Generic REST collector API for this purpose.

Steps

  1. Login to RSA G&L console as System Administrator.
  2. Navigate to Resources > Applications.
  3. Click on PagerDuty.
  4. Navigate to Collectors tab and click on Create Account Collector. Enter the details as shown below.

  5. Click Next and enter the connection details.

  6. Click Next. Select the data types that the collector will be collecting from PagerDuty

  7. Click Next. On the Account Data page, enter the following configurations.




    Request
    MethodGET
    URL/users
    Request Headers
    Acceptapplication/json
    AuthorizationToken token=${AuthenticationToken}
    Response Mappings
    AccountId$.users.*.email
    Id$.users.*.id
    Type$.users.*.type

  8. Click Next. On the Group Data page, enter the following configurations.




    Request
    MethodGET
    URL/groups
    Query Parameters
    fieldsid,name
    Request Headers
    Acceptapplication/json
    AuthorizationToken token=${AuthenticationToken}
    Response Mappings
    GroupId$.teams.*.name
    Id$.teams.*.id
    Description$.teams.*.description
  9. Click Next. On the Account Group Membership page, enter the following configurations.




    Request
    MethodGET
    URL/groups/${Id}/users
    Request Headers
    Acceptapplication/json
    AuthorizationToken token=${AuthenticationToken}
    Response Mappings
    Account Identifier$.members.*.user.id
  10. Click Next. In the Map Collector Attributes to Account Attributes, map the collected account attributes.

  11. Click Next. In the Map Collector Attributes to Account Mapping Attributes, map the user reference attribute.

  12. Click Next. In the Map Collector Attributes to Group Attributes, map the collected group attributes.

  13. Click Next. In the User Resolution Rules, map the user to account.

  14. Click Next. In the Member Account Resolution Rules, map the account.

💡 Info: This value MUST always be mapped to the Account Name for any generic REST collector.

  1. Click Finish. Use the Test function to make sure the configurations are accurate.

Entitlement Collector​

This section explains the process for configuring an entitlement collector to collect user roles and assignments from the PagerDuty application. We will use the RSA G&L Generic REST collector for this purpose.

info

Since PagerDuty does not provide an API for listing user roles, we will use a custom web service for the EDC. Refer to Custom Web Service : User Roles for more information.

Steps

  1. Login to RSA G&L console as AveksaAdmin.
  2. Navigate to Resources > Applications.
  3. Click on PagerDuty.
  4. Navigate to Collectors tab and click on Create Entitlement Collector. Enter the details as shown below.

  5. Click Next and enter the connection details as shown in Step 5 of Account Collector. 💡 Note that the Use Base URL for all API(s) will be unchecked.
  6. Click Next. Select the data types that the collector will be collecting from PagerDuty.

  7. Click Next. On the App Role Data page, enter the following configurations.


    Request
    MethodGET
    URLhttps://instance.securid.com/aveksa/custom/external_jsp/PagerDutyUserRolesv1.jsp
    Request Headers
    Acceptapplication/json
    Response Mappings
    ApplicationRoleId$.roles.*.role
    Description$.roles.*.description
  8. Click Next. On the App-Role Account Membership collection page, enter the following configurations.




    Request
    MethodGET
    URLhttps://api.pagerduty.com/users
    Request Headers
    Acceptapplication/json
    AuthorizationToken token=${AuthenticationToken}
    Response Mappings
    Application Role Id$.users.*.role
    Account Identifier$.users.*.id
  9. Click Next. In the Map Collector Attributes to App Role Attributes, map the collected account attributes.

  10. Click Next. In the Account Evaluation, map the account collector to account value.

  11. Click Next and then click Finish. Use the Test function to make sure the configurations are accurate

Application Setup​

This section explains the process of setting up an API access key in PagerDuty for use within the collections process.

Steps

  1. Login to PagerDuty console https://instance.pagerduty.com.
  2. Under Integrations, click on API Access Keys.

  3. Click on Create new API key.
  4. Provide a meaningful name. Since we are going to use this key for collections, check the Read-only API Key option.
  5. Click on Create Key.
  6. Copy the Secret and click Done.

Custom Web Service : Site Roles​

Since PagerDuty does not provide an API for listing site roles, we will use a custom JSP that will publish the site roles.

Steps

  1. Open a text editor and save the contents below as PagerDutyUserRolesv1.jsp.
<%@ page language="java" contentType="application/json" %>
<%
    String jsonData = "{"
        + "\"roles\": ["
        + "{\"role\":\"admin\",\"description\":\"Has full access to all objects and configurations in the account. Can create, update, and delete users, teams, schedules, escalation policies, services, and integrations. Can also manage user permissions.\"},"
        + "{\"role\":\"limited_user\",\"description\":\"Can view objects and configurations, but cannot make any modifications.\"},"
        + "{\"role\":\"observer\",\"description\":\"Can view objects, but cannot make any modifications.\"},"
        + "{\"role\":\"owner\",\"description\":\"Has full access to objects and configurations within their team. Can create, update, and delete users, schedules, escalation policies, and services. Can also manage user permissions within their team.\"},"
        + "{\"role\":\"read_only_user\",\"description\":\"Can view objects, but cannot make any modifications.\"},"
        + "{\"role\":\"restricted_access\",\"description\":\"Can only view incidents and alerts that they are directly involved in.\"},"
        + "{\"role\":\"read_only_limited_user\",\"description\":\"Can only view objects that they have been granted access to.\"},"
        + "{\"role\":\"user\",\"description\":\"Can view objects and configurations, but cannot make any modifications.\"}"
        + "]"
        + "}";
%>

<%
    response.setContentType("application/json");
    out.print(jsonData);
%>
  1. Login to RSA G&L console as AveksaAdmin.
  2. Navigate to Admin > User Interface.
  3. Click on Files.
  4. Select External JSP Pages and click on Upload.
  5. Select the PagerDutyUserRolesv1.jsp and click OK.
  6. Verify by accessing this URL in the browser https://instance.securid.com/aveksa/custom/external_jsp/PagerDutyUserRolesv1.jsp
  1. PagerDuty API

    ↩

item.title: Collections - Asana
item.description: Asana is a powerful project management tool that enhances team collaboration and task organization. It simplifies complex workflows, fosters clear communication, and ensures efficient task tracking. With its user-friendly interface and customizable features, Asana streamlines project management, enabling teams to achieve goals and deliver results effectively.Identities ❌ | Accounts ✔️ | Groups ✔️ | App Roles ✔️ | Entitlements ❌
item.content:encoded:
Asana is a powerful project management tool that enhances team collaboration and task organization. It simplifies complex workflows, fosters clear communication, and ensures efficient task tracking. With its user-friendly interface and customizable features, Asana streamlines project management, enabling teams to achieve goals and deliver results effectively.

Identities ❌ | Accounts ✔️ | Groups ✔️ | App Roles ✔️ | Entitlements ❌

A workspace in Asana is the top-level container for organizing and managing work. It serves as a central hub where teams, projects, tasks, and other resources are grouped together. Workspaces can be thought of as separate environments, and users can create multiple workspaces to keep different projects or teams distinct from each other. Each workspace has its own set of users, projects, and tasks.

Users are individuals who have access to an Asana workspace. They can create and manage tasks, projects, and collaborate with other team members. Users can be assigned tasks, comment on tasks, contribute to projects, and work together to achieve shared goals within the workspace.

Teams are groups of users who work together on specific projects or share common goals within a workspace. Teams allow users to collaborate more efficiently by providing a dedicated space to manage and organize tasks related to a particular function, project, or department. Teams can have their own projects, tasks, and members. Users can belong to multiple teams within a workspace, which allows for flexible collaboration across different groups.

Projects in Asana are used to organize and track tasks and work items related to a specific goal, initiative, or task list. Projects provide a structured way to manage work by breaking it down into smaller tasks and assigning responsibilities to team members. Within a project, users can create tasks, set due dates, assign owners, add descriptions, and attach files. Projects can be organized into sections or categories to further group and prioritize tasks.

Application​

Steps

  1. Login to RSA G&L console as System Administrator.
  2. Navigate to Resources > Application.
  3. Click on Create Application and select Other Application.
  4. Enter the details as shown below.
  5. Click Finish.

Account Collector​

This section explains the process for configuring an account collector for the Asana application. We will use the RSA G&L Generic REST collector API for this purpose.

Steps

  1. Login to RSA G&L console as System Administrator.
  2. Navigate to Resources > Applications.
  3. Click on Asana.
  4. Navigate to Collectors tab and click on Create Account Collector. Enter the details as shown below.

  5. Click Next and enter the connection details.

  6. Click Next. Select the data types that the collector will be collecting from Asana

  7. Click Next. On the Account Data page, enter the following configurations.



    Request
    MethodGET
    URL/users
    Query Parameters
    opt_fieldsemail
    Request Headers
    Acceptapplication/json
    AuthorizationBearer ${AuthenticationToken}
    Response Mappings
    AccountId$.data.*.email
    Id$.data.*.gid

  8. Click Next. On the Group Data page, enter the following configurations.




    Request
    MethodGET
    URL/workspaces/WORKSPACE_ID/teams
    Query Parameters
    opt_fieldsname,description,visibility
    Request Headers
    Acceptapplication/json
    AuthorizationBearer ${AuthenticationToken}
    Response Mappings
    GroupId$.data.*.name
    Id$.data.*.gid
    Description$.data.*.description
    Visibility$.data.*.visibility
  9. Click Next. On the Account Group Membership page, enter the following configurations.




    Request
    MethodGET
    URL/teams/${Id}/team_memberships
    Query Parameters
    opt_fieldsuser
    Request Headers
    Acceptapplication/json
    AuthorizationBearer ${AuthenticationToken}
    Response Mappings
    Account Identifier$.data.*.user.gid
  10. Click Next. In the Map Collector Attributes to Account Attributes, map the collected account attributes.

  11. Click Next. In the Map Collector Attributes to Account Mapping Attributes, map the user reference attribute.

  12. Click Next. In the Map Collector Attributes to Group Attributes, map the collected group attributes.

  13. Click Next. In the User Resolution Rules, map the user to account.

  14. Click Next. In the Member Account Resolution Rules, map the account.

💡 Info: This value MUST always be mapped to the Account Name for any generic REST collector.

  1. Click Finish. Use the Test function to make sure the configurations are accurate.

Entitlement Collector​

This section explains the process for configuring an entitlement collector to collect projects and assignments from the Asana application. We will use the RSA G&L Generic REST collector for this purpose.

Steps

  1. Login to RSA G&L console as AveksaAdmin.
  2. Navigate to Resources > Applications.
  3. Click on Asana.
  4. Navigate to Collectors tab and click on Create Entitlement Collector. Enter the details as shown below.

  5. Click Next and enter the connection details as shown in Step 5 of Account Collector.
  6. Click Next. Select the data types that the collector will be collecting from Asana.

  7. Click Next. On the App Role Data page, enter the following configurations.



    Request
    MethodGET
    URL/projects
    Query Parameters
    opt_fieldsname,notes,owner
    Request Headers
    Acceptapplication/json
    AuthorizationBearer ${AuthenticationToken}
    Response Mappings
    ApplicationRoleId$.data.*.name
    Id$.data.*.gid
    Description$.data.*.notes
    Owner$.data.*.owner.gid
  8. Click Next. On the App-Role Account Membership collection page, enter the following configurations.




    Request
    MethodGET
    URL/projects
    Query Parameters
    opt_fieldsmembers
    Request Headers
    Acceptapplication/json
    AuthorizationBearer ${AuthenticationToken}
    Response Mappings
    Application Role Id$.data.*.gid
    Account Identifier$.data.*.members.*.gid
  9. Click Next. In the Map Collector Attributes to App Role Attributes, map the collected account attributes.

  10. Click Next. In the Account Evaluation, map the account collector to account value.

  11. Click Next and then click Finish. Use the Test function to make sure the configurations are accurate

Application Setup​

This section explains the process of setting up a service account in Asana for use within the collections process.

Steps

  1. Login to Asana Developer console, https://app.asana.com/0/my-apps.
  2. Click on Create new app.

  3. Provide a suitable name, and click Create app

  4. Copy the Client ID and Secret.

  5. Click on Configure > OAuth
  6. Add the redirect URL for the G&L instance https://instance.securid.com:443/aveksa/oauth/callback

  7. Navigate to Manage Distribution under Test & Distribute section. Select Any Workspace and click Save Changes.

OAuth2 Configuration​

This section explains the process of setting up RSA G&L ADC with token authentication.

Steps

  1. Login to RSA G&L console as System Administrator.
  2. Create or Edit a generic REST ADC that will be using OAuth2 authentication.
  3. On the Connection Details page, configure the following.


    Request
    Client ID-
    Client Secret-
    Authentication URL https://app.asana.com/-/oauth_authorize
    Access Token URLhttps://app.asana.com/-/oauth_token
    Scopedefault
  4. Click on Get OAuth 2.0 Access Token.
  5. Click on Proceed For Authorization.

  6. Login to Asana account.


  7. The below message indicates that RSA G&L has successfully acquired a token.

  8. Click Close.
  9. Save the collector configuration & test.
  1. API Reference

    ↩

item.title: Collections - Dynatrace
item.description: Dynatrace is a software intelligence company that specializes in application performance management (APM) and observability solutions for modern cloud environments. Its platform provides real-time insights into the performance of applications and the underlying infrastructure that supports them. Dynatrace's primary goal is to help organizations ensure the optimal functioning of their digital services and applications, especially in complex and dynamic environments.Identities ❌ | Accounts ✔️ | Groups ✔️ | App Roles ❌ | Entitlements ❌
item.content:encoded:
Dynatrace is a software intelligence company that specializes in application performance management (APM) and observability solutions for modern cloud environments. Its platform provides real-time insights into the performance of applications and the underlying infrastructure that supports them. Dynatrace's primary goal is to help organizations ensure the optimal functioning of their digital services and applications, especially in complex and dynamic environments.

Identities ❌ | Accounts ✔️ | Groups ✔️ | App Roles ❌ | Entitlements ❌

Application​

Steps

  1. Login to RSA G&L console as System Administrator.
  2. Navigate to Resources > Application.
  3. Click on Create Application and select Other Application.
  4. Enter the details as shown below.
  5. Click Finish.

Account Collector​

This section explains the process for configuring an account collector for the Dynatrace application. We will use the RSA G&L Generic REST collector API for this purpose.

Steps

  1. Login to RSA G&L console as System Administrator.
  2. Navigate to Resources > Applications.
  3. Click on Dynatrace.
  4. Navigate to Collectors tab and click on Create Account Collector. Enter the details as shown below.

  5. Click Next and enter the connection details. Refer to Token Authentication for more information on authentication configuration.

  6. Click Next. Select the data types that the collector will be collecting from Dynatrace

  7. Click Next. On the Account Data page, enter the following configurations.



    Request
    MethodGET
    URL/users
    Request Headers
    Acceptapplication/json
    AuthorizationBearer ${AuthenticationToken}
    Response Mappings
    AccountId$.items.*.email
    Id$.items.*.uid
    UserStatus$.items.*.userStatus
    LastLogin$.items.*.userLoginMetadata.lastSuccessfulLogin

  8. Click Next. On the Group Data page, enter the following configurations.



    Request
    MethodGET
    URL/groups
    Request Headers
    Acceptapplication/json
    AuthorizationBearer ${AuthenticationToken}
    Response Mappings
    GroupId$.items.*.name
    Id$.items.*.uuid
    Description$.items.*.description
  9. Click Next. On the Account Group Membership page, enter the following configurations.



    Request
    MethodGET
    URL/groups/${Id}/users
    Request Headers
    Acceptapplication/json
    AuthorizationBearer ${AuthenticationToken}
    Response Mappings
    Account Identifier$.data.*.id
  10. Click Next. In the Map Collector Attributes to Account Attributes, map the collected account attributes.

  11. Click Next. In the Map Collector Attributes to Account Mapping Attributes, map the user reference attribute.

  12. Click Next. In the Map Collector Attributes to Group Attributes, map the collected group attributes.

  13. Click Next. In the User Resolution Rules, map the user to account.

  14. Click Next. In the Member Account Resolution Rules, map the account.

💡 Info: This value MUST always be mapped to the Account Name for any generic REST collector.

  1. Click Finish. Use the Test function to make sure the configurations are accurate.

Application Setup​

This section explains the process of setting up a service account in Dynatrace for use within the collections process.

Steps

  1. Login to Dynatrace Account Management console https://myaccount.dynatrace.com/account/home.
  2. Under Identity & access management, click on OAuth clients.

  3. Click on Create client.

  4. Provide a valid (existing / Active) user email address and a meaningful description. We will restrict permissions to view user and groups data.

  5. Click Create client.

  6. Copy the client ID, client secret and account ID (portion after urn:daccount:).

Token Authentication​

This section explains the process of setting up RSA G&L ADC with token authentication.

Steps

  1. Login to RSA G&L console as System Administrator.
  2. Create or Edit a generic REST ADC that will be using Token authentication.
  3. On the Connection Details page, configure the following.



    Request
    MethodPOST
    Token URLhttps://sso.dynatrace.com/sso/oauth2/token
    UsernameClient ID
    PasswordClient Secret
    Request Headers
    Acceptapplication/json
    Content-Typeapplication/x-www-form-urlencoded
    Request Body
    grant_type=client_credentials&client_id=${TokenUsername}&client_secret=${TokenPassword}&scope=account-idm-read
    Response Mappings
    Response Path$.access_token
  4. Save the collector configuration & test.
  1. Dynatrace API

    ↩

item.title: Collections - Zoho CRM
item.description: Zoho CRM empowers businesses with a comprehensive customer relationship management solution. It streamlines sales, marketing, and support processes, fostering efficient customer engagement. Through a user-friendly interface, it offers tools for lead management, analytics, and automation, enabling businesses to build lasting relationships, drive growth, and make informed decisions.Identities ❌ | Accounts ✔️ | Groups ✔️ | App Roles ✔️ | Entitlements ❌
item.content:encoded:
Zoho CRM empowers businesses with a comprehensive customer relationship management solution. It streamlines sales, marketing, and support processes, fostering efficient customer engagement. Through a user-friendly interface, it offers tools for lead management, analytics, and automation, enabling businesses to build lasting relationships, drive growth, and make informed decisions.

Identities ❌ | Accounts ✔️ | Groups ✔️ | App Roles ✔️ | Entitlements ❌

Users are individuals who have access to your Zoho CRM account. Each user is associated with a profile and can be assigned specific roles and user group memberships. The combination of profile, roles, and user group memberships defines a user's overall access and permissions.

Profiles serve as the foundation of the hierarchy. Each user is assigned a profile that determines their basic access settings, available features, and modules they can access. Profiles are templates that define a user's general CRM experience.

Roles build upon profiles by adding specific permissions and access rights. Roles allow you to control what records and modules a user can access or manipulate. Users with the same profile but different roles can have different levels of access within the CRM.

User Groups are collections of users who share similar roles, responsibilities, or access requirements. User groups allow for efficient management of permissions. Roles and profiles can be assigned to user groups, making it easier to apply consistent access settings to multiple users.

Application​

Steps

  1. Login to RSA G&L console as System Administrator.
  2. Navigate to Resources > Application.
  3. Click on Create Application and select Other Application.
  4. Enter the details as shown below.
  5. Click Finish.

Account Collector​

This section explains the process for configuring an account collector for the Zoho CRM application. We will use the RSA G&L Generic REST collector API for this purpose.

Steps

  1. Login to RSA G&L console as System Administrator.
  2. Navigate to Resources > Applications.
  3. Click on Zoho CRM.
  4. Navigate to Collectors tab and click on Create Account Collector. Enter the details as shown below.

  5. Click Next and enter the connection details.

  6. Click Next. Select the data types that the collector will be collecting from Zoho CRM

  7. Click Next. On the Account Data page, enter the following configurations.




    Request
    MethodGET
    URL/users
    Request Headers
    Acceptapplication/json
    AuthorizationBearer ${AuthenticationToken}
    Response Mappings
    AccountId$.users.*.email
    Id$.users.*.id
    Type$.users.*.status

  8. Click Next. On the Group Data page, enter the following configurations.




    Request
    MethodGET
    URL/settings/user_groups
    Request Headers
    Acceptapplication/json
    AuthorizationBearer ${AuthenticationToken}
    Response Mappings
    GroupId$.user_groups.*.name
    Id$.user_groups.*.id
    Description$.user_groups.*.description
  9. Click Next. On the Account Group Membership page, enter the following configurations.




    Request
    MethodGET
    URL/settings/user_groups/${Id}/sources
    Request Headers
    Acceptapplication/json
    AuthorizationBearer ${AuthenticationToken}
    Response Mappings
    Account Identifier$.sources.*.source.id
  10. Click Next. In the Map Collector Attributes to Account Attributes, map the collected account attributes.

  11. Click Next. In the Map Collector Attributes to Account Mapping Attributes, map the user reference attribute.

  12. Click Next. In the Map Collector Attributes to Group Attributes, map the collected group attributes.

  13. Click Next. In the User Resolution Rules, map the user to account.

  14. Click Next. In the Member Account Resolution Rules, map the account.

💡 Info: This value MUST always be mapped to the Account Name for any generic REST collector.

  1. Click Finish. Use the Test function to make sure the configurations are accurate.

Entitlement Collector​

This section explains the process for configuring an entitlement collector to collect user roles and assignments from the Zoho CRM application. We will use the RSA G&L Generic REST collector for this purpose.

Steps

  1. Login to RSA G&L console as AveksaAdmin.
  2. Navigate to Resources > Applications.
  3. Click on Zoho CRM.
  4. Navigate to Collectors tab and click on Create Entitlement Collector. Enter the details as shown below.

  5. Click Next and enter the connection details as shown in Step 5 of Account Collector.
  6. Click Next. Select the data types that the collector will be collecting from Zoho CRM.

  7. Click Next. On the App Role Data page, enter the following configurations.



    Request
    MethodGET
    URL/settings/roles
    Request Headers
    Acceptapplication/json
    AuthorizationBearer ${AuthenticationToken}
    Response Mappings
    ApplicationRoleId$.roles.*.name
    Id$.roles.*.id
    Description$.roles.*.description
  8. Click Next. On the App-Role Account Membership collection page, enter the following configurations.




    Request
    MethodGET
    URL/users
    Request Headers
    Acceptapplication/json
    AuthorizationBearer ${AuthenticationToken}
    Response Mappings
    Application Role Id$.users.*.role.id
    Account Identifier$.users.*.id
  9. Click Next. In the Map Collector Attributes to App Role Attributes, map the collected account attributes.

  10. Click Next. In the Account Evaluation, map the account collector to account value.

  11. Click Next and then click Finish. Use the Test function to make sure the configurations are accurate.

Application Setup​

This section explains the process of setting up an API access key in Zoho CRM for use within the collections process.

Steps

  1. Login to Zoho API console, https://api-console.zoho.com/.
  2. Click on Add Client.
  3. Select the Server-based Applications client type.

  4. Provide a suitable name, and the redirect URI

  5. Copy the Client ID and Secret.

OAuth2 Configuration​

This section explains the process of setting up RSA G&L ADC with OAuth2 authentication.

Steps

  1. Login to RSA G&L console as System Administrator.
  2. Create or Edit a generic REST ADC that will be using OAuth2 authentication.
  3. On the Connection Details page, configure the following. Refer to Authorization Request for information regarding access_type and prompt parameters in the request.


    Request
    Client ID-
    Client Secret-
    Authentication URLhttps://accounts.zoho.com/oauth/v2/auth
    Access Token URLhttps://accounts.zoho.com/oauth/v2/token
    ScopeZohoCRM.settings.user_groups.READ ZohoCRM.settings.roles.READ ZohoCRM.users.READ&access_type=offline&prompt=consent
  4. Click on Get OAuth 2.0 Access Token.
  5. Click on Proceed For Authorization.

  6. Login to Zoho CRM account.

  7. The below message indicates that RSA G&L has successfully acquired a token.

  8. Click Close.
  9. Save the collector configuration. Use the Test function to make sure the configurations are accurate
  1. Zoho CRM V5 APIs

    ↩

item.title: Collections - PingOne
item.description: PingOne is an identity and access management (IAM) solution by Ping Identity. It offers secure user authentication, authorization, and single sign-on across applications. With advanced features like multifactor authentication and adaptive security, PingOne ensures streamlined access control while enhancing user experience and safeguarding sensitive data.Identities ❌ | Accounts ✔️ | Groups ✔️ | App Roles ❌ | Entitlements ❌
item.content:encoded:
PingOne is an identity and access management (IAM) solution by Ping Identity. It offers secure user authentication, authorization, and single sign-on across applications. With advanced features like multifactor authentication and adaptive security, PingOne ensures streamlined access control while enhancing user experience and safeguarding sensitive data.

Identities ❌ | Accounts ✔️ | Groups ✔️ | App Roles ❌ | Entitlements ❌

Application​

Steps

  1. Login to RSA G&L console as System Administrator.
  2. Navigate to Resources > Application.
  3. Click on Create Application and select Other Application.
  4. Enter the details as shown below.
  5. Click Finish.

Account Collector​

This section explains the process for configuring an account collector for the PingOne application. We will use the RSA G&L Generic REST collector API for this purpose.

Steps

  1. Login to RSA G&L console as System Administrator.
  2. Navigate to Resources > Applications.
  3. Click on PingOne.
  4. Navigate to Collectors tab and click on Create Account Collector. Enter the details as shown below.

  5. Click Next and enter the connection details. PingOne supports both OAuth and API key based authentication. We will token authentication for our collectors in this article. Refer to Token Authentication section.

  6. Click Next. Select the data types that the collector will be collecting from PingOne

  7. Click Next. On the Account Data page, enter the following configurations.



    Request
    MethodGET
    URL/users
    Request Headers
    Acceptapplication/json
    AuthorizationBearer ${AuthenticationToken}
    Response Mappings
    AccountId$._embedded.users.*.email
    Id$._embedded.users.*.id
    Status$._embedded.users.*.verifyStatus
    IsEnabled$._embedded.users.*.enabled
    UserName$._embedded.users.*.username

  8. Click Next. On the Group Data page, enter the following configurations.



    Request
    MethodGET
    URL/groups
    Request Headers
    Acceptapplication/json
    AuthorizationBearer ${AuthenticationToken}
    Response Mappings
    GroupId$._embedded.groups.*.name
    Id$._embedded.groups.*.id
    Description$._embedded.groups.*.description
    Owner$._embedded.groups.*.customData.groupOwner
  9. Click Next. On the Account Group Membership page, enter the following configurations.



    Request
    MethodGET
    URL/users/${Id}
    Query Parameters
    includememberOfGroupIDs
    Request Headers
    Acceptapplication/json
    AuthorizationBearer ${AuthenticationToken}
    Response Mappings
    Group Identifier$.memberOfGroupIDs.*
  10. Click Next. On the Subgroup Membership page, enter the following configurations.



    Request
    MethodGET
    URL/groups/${Id}/memberOfGroups
    Request Headers
    Acceptapplication/json
    AuthorizationBearer ${AuthenticationToken}
    Response Mappings
    Subgroup Identifier$._embedded.groupMemberships.*.id
  11. Click Next. In the Map Collector Attributes to Account Attributes, map the collected account attributes.

  12. Click Next. In the Map Collector Attributes to Account Mapping Attributes, map the user reference attribute.

  13. Click Next. In the Map Collector Attributes to Group Attributes, map the collected group attributes.

  14. Click Next. In the User Resolution Rules, map the user to account.

  15. Click Next. In the Member Account Resolution Rules, map the account attribute.

  16. Click Next. In the Sub-group Resolution Rules, map the group attribute.

  17. Click Next. In the Group Owner Resolution Rules, map the group attribute.

  18. Click Finish. Use the Test function to make sure the configurations are accurate.

Application Setup​

This section explains the process of setting up a service account in PingOne for use within the collections process.

Steps

  1. Login to PingOne console https://console.pingone.com/index.html
  2. Navigate to Connections > Applications.

  3. Click on Add Applications.

  4. Enter a suitable name and description and select application type as Worker.

  5. Click on Save.
